meaning: adj.未受制止的,未经检查的,未加抑制的,未选中的
examples: If left unchecked, weeds will flourish.
The fire was allowed to burn unchecked.
The rise in violent crime must not go unchecked.
But with the chaos unchecked, the question is how safe it is for Thais.
Clinton voiced strong support for NATO action and said the Serbian "atrocities" could not go unchecked.
